results.rendered_html: vāsava—dattā f. N. of various women (esp. of the wife of Udayana, king of Vatsa and daughter of king Caṇḍa-mahā-sena of Ujjayinī [ Kathās. ] or of king Pradyota [ Ratnāv. ], to whom she offered herself after having been betrothed by her father to Saṃjaya [ Mālatīm. ]; and of the heroine of Subandhu's novel, who is represented to have been betrothed by her father to Puṣpa-ketu, but carried off by Kandarpa-ketu)
